data.title: Nasdaq, Russell 2000 scale new record highs as Wall Street heads for strong week
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday was largely higher, with the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) hitting a new record high on a boost from Apple (AAPL). Small-cap stocks grabbed some of the spotlight after their barometer, the Russell 2000 (RTY), also scaled a new all-time peak.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.2% in morning trade. The benchmark gauge was headed for solid weekly gains, as market participants finally received an interest rate cut from the Federal Reserve after nearly nine months of inaction on monetary policy. 

The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was +0.4%, helped by a +1.1% rise in Apple (AAPL). The tech giant's new iPhone 17 became generally available to the public today, with early indicators showing stronger-than-expected demand for the product.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.2%. Meanwhile, the Russell 2000 (RTY) had reversed course, -0.2%. The benchmark for small-cap stocks on Thursday ended a 967-trading-day streak without a new all-time high, its second longest ever, according to Bespoke Investment Group. 

For traders, the focus on Friday was on a scheduled phone call between U.S. President Donald Trump and China's Xi Jinping. The leaders of the world's two biggest economies are expected to discuss a host of topics, including trade, TikTok, and Nvidia (NVDA). 

Over in the fixed-income market, yields stabilized somewhat after a two-day bond selloff drove them higher. Despite the Fed's decision to cut its key policy rate by 25 basis points on Wednesday and a more dovish projection on rates for the rest of the year, the selling was ignited by chair Jerome Powell's more hawkish speech. 

"Both the dots and the statement were more dovish than we expected. However, Chair Powell’s press conference was more hawkish. He downplayed the dots and characterized today’s move as a risk management cut, a characterization which casts doubt on whether today’s move is necessarily the start of a long easing cycle," JPMorgan's Michael Feroli said on Wednesday. 

The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last up 1 basis point to 4.12%, while the more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was little changed at 3.78%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: OpenAI poaches Apple's hardware talent, manufacturing partners for new device: report
data.description:
OpenAI is going after some of Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) hardware designers and even its manufacturing partners, as the ChatGPT maker prepares to launch its own devices, The Information reported. 

The Sam Altman-led OpenAI has hired some of Apple's design, manufacturing and supply chain teams, as well as some of Apple's AI researchers, the news outlet added, citing a review of LinkedIn profiles. 

The company has also signed a deal with Luxshare — which assembles iPhones and AirPods in China — to assemble at least one of OpenAI's future devices, the news outlet added, citing multiple people with knowledge of the matter. OpenAI has also spoken to Goertek, which assembles AirPods, HomePods and Apple Watches, for component supply for future OpenAI devices, the people added. 

Though not much is known about the device, Altman and former Apple exec Jony Ive, who joined OpenAI earlier this year, previewed the device in May with OpenAI employees. One of the products that OpenAI has spoken with its manufacturing partners about resembles a smart speaker, sans a display, The Information reported, citing sources.

OpenAI, which is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), completed its roughly $6.5B all-stock deal to acquire Ive's hardware startup, io, in July.

Other considerations include glasses, digital voice recorder and wearable pin. However, Altman told the staff that the device is not a pair of glasses, and that Ive had been skeptical about building something to wear on the body.

“We’re not going to ship 100 million devices literally on day one,” Altman told his staff in May. However, he said that OpenAI would ship that large number of high-quality devices “faster than any company has ever shipped 100 million of something new before.” Altman said the aim is to release a device by late next year.

Apple and OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: XXII, FDX, UPS, HESM, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Friday, extending record-setting gains from the prior session as investors absorbed the Federal Reserve’s rate cut.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • 22nd Century Group (NASDAQ:XXII) +10% - Shares surged after the company announced it is now debt-free following repayment of $3.9M in senior secured debt. The move eliminates cash interest and amortization expense, lowering ongoing costs. Backed by new growth capital from its Series A preferred offering, XXII plans to expand its very low nicotine (VLN) tobacco leaf inventory, supporting production of 1M+ cartons of VLN products.
  • FedEx (NYSE:FDX) +3% - Shares climbed after the company beat FQ1 earnings estimates. For FY2026, FedEx projects 4%–6% revenue growth and EPS of $17.20–$19.00 (midpoint $18.10) vs. $18.36 consensus. “Our strategic initiatives, paired with our operational data platform moving 17M packages daily, position us to serve customers in any environment and drive long-term value,” said CEO Raj Subramaniam.
  • United Parcel Service (NYSE:UPS) +2% - Stock moved up, in tandem with FedEx's better-than-expected earnings report for the quarter, which lifted sentiment across the logistics sector. Separately, UPS confirmed it has scrapped its planned acquisition of Mexican courier Estafeta, stating that unmet closing conditions could not be resolved.

Biggest stock losers

  • Scholastic Corporation (NASDAQ:SCHL) -14% - Shares tumbled after missing FQ1 expectations, with Education Solutions revenue down 28% and Entertainment down 18%. Despite the miss, the company reaffirmed FY2026 guidance as adjusted EBITDA loss improved 8% to $55.7M, helped by cost cuts and stronger Children’s Publishing sales.
  • Hess Midstream (HESM) -8% - Shares slipped after the company cut FY2025 guidance and flagged flat 2026 adjusted EBITDA due to Chevron (CVX) reducing Bakken rig activity from four to three rigs in Q4. Gas throughput is now expected at 455M–465M cf/day and gas processing at 440M–450M cf/day, with Q3 earnings at the low end of guidance. Oil volumes are seen plateauing in 2026, while gas throughput growth is expected to continue through 2027. Despite near-term pressure, HESM reaffirmed plans for at least 5% annual distribution growth through 2027, supported by rising free cash flow.
  • Orla Mining (ORLA) -7% - Shares dipped after Newmont (NEM) sold its entire 13.3% stake (43.2M shares) in the company on the Toronto Stock Exchange at US$10.14/share, raising US$439M (C$605M). Newmont said it valued its partnership with Orla, citing projects like Camino Rojo and this year’s Musselwhite acquisition.
  • Lennar (NYSE:LEN) -4% - Shares of the home construction company slipped after Q3 results missed on both revenue and EPS. Net earnings fell ~50% Y/Y, with home sales revenue down 9% and gross margin contracting to 17.5% from 22.5% a year ago. For Q4, Lennar guided to 20K–21K new orders at an average sales price of $380K–$390K.

data.title: Research firm Citizens thinks Google has won the AI browser war before it even began
data.description:
Analysts at Citizens have reiterated their bullish rating on tech giant Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and have hiked the price target on its stock following the company's announcement that it has integrated AI into Chrome across multiple levels.

The research firm has noted that Google is early to market with an agentic browser and has distribution channels.

"While Perplexity's Comet was the first to market with an agentic browser, we are impressed that Google quickly matched those capabilities," the research firm said.

"Simply put, this is the first step toward agents beginning to help internet browsers complete actions and find and summarize information at the browser level, enabling AI to bring tasks and information instead of the other way around," they added. 

Citizens also pointed out that Gemini AI in Chrome, which works across apps like Calendar, YouTube, and Maps, is different from AI built for its other properties like Gmail, which is a Google Workspace product. Therefore, they believe the company's access to its broader ecosystem makes its AI "a more complete digital assistant."

GOOGL has a PT of $290, hiked from $250, implying an upside of 15.1%.

data.title: SEC will propose reducing frequency of earnings reports - report
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission will propose a change to semiannual corporate earnings reports, rather than the quarterly reports now required, the agency's head said on Friday in a broadcast interview.

SEC Chairman Paul Atkins said he has spoken with President Donald Trump about the matter. Proposing the change "would be a good way forward, and then we’ll consider that, and move forward after that," he said in an interview on CNBC.

Earlier this week, Trump suggested that the reporting frequency for publicly traded companies be changed to once every six months instead of once every three months. "This will save money and allow managers to focus on properly running their companies," Trump said in a post on Truth Social. Atkins welcomed the posting.

If a change to the reporting rules is approved, then it will be up to companies to decide if they want to report semiannually or quarterly. “For the sake of shareholders and public companies, the market can decide what the proper cadence is,” Atkins said on CNBC.

Atkins pointed out that foreign private issuers follow a semiannual reporting schedule. "You have to realize that right now, semi-annual reporting is no stranger to our markets, foreign private issuers do it right now," he said.

Proponents of quarterly earnings say that shareholders need the frequent updates to make investment decisions, and frequent disclosures make markets more efficient.

Atkins said that semiannual earnings disclosures would still be supplemented by 8-K filings, which are required for material developments in companies' operations.

Critics of the quarterly reporting schedule say that it leads to a focus on short-term results rather than managing businesses for the longer term. "There's been a lot of complaints over the years about  'short-term-ism' over the years, so let's see what we can do to address it," Atkins said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Medtronic gets FDA nod for the Altaviva device
data.description:
• Medtronic (NYSE:MDT) said that it has received U.S. FDA approval for its bladder control device, Altaviva. 
  • The implantable tibial neuromodulation therapy is inserted near the ankle and designed to treat urge urinary incontinence.

data.title: Paramount Skydance may bid $22 to $24 a share for Warner Bros. Discovery - CNBC
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) may offer $22 to $24 a share for Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD).

An offer may consist of 20%-30% in Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) stock and the remainder in cash, according to CNBC's David Faber, who cited speculation.

Shares of Warner Bros. Discovery rose 2.1%. 

The latest update come after CNBC's Faber said on Tuesday that a potential Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) may take a bit longer than expected.

data.title: Samsung's latest HBM product receives validation from Nvidia: report
data.description:
Samsung's (OTCPK:SSNLF) fifth-generation, 12-layer HBM3E has passed Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) qualification tests, meaning it can now become a critical supplier for Nvidia's most powerful artificial intelligence accelerators, according to The Korea Economic Daily.

Samsung's HBM3E had already passed qualification tests for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) MI350, according to the report, which cited people familiar with the matter. It can now be used with Nvidia's B300 Blackwell Ultra GPU. Nvidia had already approved 12-layer HBM3E chips from SK Hynix and Micron Technology (MU).

However, after achieving this milestone, Samsung must now perfect HBM4, which is slated to appear in Nvidia's next generation of GPUs, the Vera Rubin. The Vera Rubin will succeed the Blackwell and is expected for release during the second half of 2026.

Samsung plans to send some HBM4 samples to Nvidia as soon as this month, the report said. The South Korean company is in talks to supply HBM4 to Broadcom (AVGO) and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) as well. Samsung partnered with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) to develop its HMB4 offering.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson reports positive results from multiple myeloma trial
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) announced promising results from a mid-stage trial evaluating a combination of Tecvayli (teclistamab) and Darzalex Faspro (daratumumab and hyaluronidase) for the treatment of multiple myeloma.
  • The study, which involved 49 patients placed in three groups, showed an overall response of 100% after the first round of treatment.
  • By the sixth treatment cycle, nearly 86% of patients had achieved a complete or very deep response, with no signs of detectable disease.
  • JNJ added that safety results confirmed the therapy was generally well tolerated.

data.title: KalVista, Ionis/Sobi among latest to win EU nod for rare disease drugs
data.description:
KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) and Ionis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:IONS) announced on Friday that the European Commission has granted final approval for two of their treatments targeting rare genetic disorders.

Cambridge, Massachusetts-based KalVista (NASDAQ:KALV) said that the EC and Swiss medical regulator, the Swiss Agency for Therapeutic Products, approved its lead product, Ekterly, for hereditary angioedema, a rare disorder characterized by tissue swelling.

Specifically, the approvals allow the marketing of Ekterly as an oral on-demand treatment for acute attacks of HAE in patients aged 12 years and older.

Meanwhile, Ionis (NASDAQ:IONS) and its partner Sobi (OTCPK:BIOVF) (OTCPK:SWOBY) announced the EU approval of its anti-lipid agent Tryngolza as a treatment with diet for adults with familial chylomicronemia syndrome, a rare condition marked by high triglycerides.

The decision follows a positive opinion an expert panel of the EU drug regulator granted in July based on a Phase 3 Balance study, in which Tryngolza helped cut patients’ triglyceride levels with a statistically significant effect over six months.
